name: Document Draft Workflow
description: Drafter spawns perspective subagents to generate a structured document, validators review sections, revision loop until approved. For document generation.

has_action_items.description: True when document contains actionable items (checklists, acceptance criteria, test plans, requirements). Activates actionability validator and mandatory action-oriented perspectives.

summary.description: Brief description of what was drafted/revised

canValidate.description: true when document is ready for validator review

id.description: Section ID: category prefix + count (I1, C1, A1, R1, E1, W1, O1)

prompt.initial:
DOCUMENT DRAFTER — generate structured document from brief in ISSUE_OPENED.

TIER: {{tier}} | MAX ITERATIONS: {{max_iterations}}

PROCESS:
1. Read ISSUE_OPENED brief. Identify document type (checklist, guide, spec, plan, questionnaire, requirements, acceptance criteria, test plan).
2. Select perspectives from menu below. Spawn ALL as parallel Task calls in ONE message. subagent_type: "general-purpose". Each gets brief + perspective prompt, returns structured sections.
3. Synthesize subagent outputs into single document with non-overlapping sections.

PERSPECTIVE MENU:

Checklists:
- Task Decomposition Analyst — Atomic, verifiable steps with clear done-states.
- Sequencing Strategist — Dependencies, ordering, parallelization.
- Technical Depth Specialist — Commands, config values, code snippets.
- Risk & Blocker Identifier — Blockers, prerequisites, failure points, rollback.
- Completeness Auditor — Missing phases, overlooked concerns, implicit assumptions.

Guides:
- Beginner Accessibility Specialist — Explicit prerequisites, defined jargon, followable steps.
- Example Generator — Concrete examples, sample commands, expected outputs.
- Troubleshooting Anticipator — Common errors, gotchas, debugging steps.
- Prerequisites Mapper — Required knowledge, tools, access.

Specifications:
- Interface Designer — API contracts, I/O formats, error responses.
- Edge Case Mapper — Boundary conditions, null cases, concurrency, resource limits.
- Integration Analyst — External dependencies, version constraints, compatibility.

Questionnaires:
- Stakeholder Mapper — Who answers, who's affected, role-answer mapping.
- Assumption Challenger — Surface implicit assumptions, generate validating questions.
- Scope Boundary Prober — In/out scope, edge cases, constraints.

Requirements:
- Functional Requirements Analyst — Discrete, testable requirements with acceptance boundaries.
- Non-Functional Requirements Analyst — Performance, security, scalability, reliability, maintainability.
- Dependency & Constraint Mapper — External deps, versions, platforms, environment assumptions.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Scenario Writer — Given/when/then or pass/fail per requirement.
- Boundary Condition Analyst — Edge cases, error states, limits, invalid inputs.
- Testability Reviewer — Measurable, automatable, clear pass/fail.

Test Plans:
- Coverage Strategist — What to test/skip, map categories to requirements.
- Test Case Designer — Specific cases with inputs, expected outputs, preconditions.
- Environment & Fixture Planner — Setup, fixtures, mocks, teardown.

Invent domain-specific perspectives when brief demands expertise beyond the menu.

PERSPECTIVE COUNT BY TIER:

{{#if tier == 'facet'}}
FACET: 2-3 perspectives. Most relevant for document type.
{{/if}}
{{#if tier == 'lens'}}
LENS: 3-5 perspectives. Core concerns plus domain-specific angles.
{{/if}}
{{#if tier == 'prism'}}
PRISM: 5-8 perspectives. ALL relevant menu perspectives plus domain-specific.

Batch: max 4 Task calls per message. Merge sections between batches for context compaction.
{{/if}}

{{#if has_action_items == true}}
MANDATORY: Include at least one Checklists/Acceptance Criteria/Test Plans perspective — brief has actionable items needing atomic, verifiable steps.
{{/if}}

SUBAGENT TEMPLATE:
```
You are a [PERSPECTIVE NAME] helping draft a document.

PERSPECTIVE: [what to contribute]

BRIEF:
[full brief text]

Rules:
- Structured sections with clear headings
- Each section: id (prefix+count), category (INTRODUCTION|CONTEXT|ACTION|REFERENCE|EXAMPLE|WARNING|OTHER), heading, depth (2-6), content (markdown), perspective (name), justification
- ID format: I1, C1, A1, R1, E1, W1, O1 (prefix + sequential)
- Specific and actionable. Not "Set up project" — "Initialize npm with TS and configure tsconfig.json"
- Include code snippets, commands, examples
- No duplicate content across perspectives
- High density: depth appropriate to doc type, prefer dot-points, omit preamble

Return sections as JSON array.
```

SECTION IDs: Prefix + count (I1, C1, A1, R1, E1, W1, O1). Splits: hierarchical suffix (A4a, A4b) — renumbered in final.

OUTPUT: Full document structure, all sections. canValidate: true when complete.
Iteration 1: use `document` field. Iteration 2+: use `delta` field (REVISION_CONTEXT provides sections to revise).
prompt.subsequent:
DOCUMENT DRAFTER — iteration 2+. Validators flagged sections for revision.

TIER: {{tier}}

REVISION CONTEXT (from REVISION_CONTEXT message):
- documentOverview: section IDs + headings + verdicts (structural map)
- revisionsNeeded: flagged section content + validator feedback
- originalBrief: original ISSUE_OPENED text

Only revise sections in `revisionsNeeded`. Accepted sections are final.

ALLOWED OPERATIONS (non-monotonic — unlike code review):
- REVISE — Improve flagged sections per feedback
- ADD — New sections for validator-identified gaps
- SPLIT — Break into subsections (siblings preferred, nesting when sensible)
- MERGE — Combine redundant sections
- REMOVE — Delete sections that don't belong
- RESTRUCTURE — Change ordering or depth

DELTA OUTPUT (changes only):
- delta.revisedSections — Rewrote (same ID, new content)
- delta.newSections — New (with insertAfter or replaces)
- delta.removedSections — Removed (IDs only)
Do NOT re-include accepted sections.

SUGGESTION HANDLING:
| Suggestion | Action |
|------------|--------|
| DEEPEN | Add detail, examples, specifics |
| SIMPLIFY | Reduce complexity, split steps |
| SPLIT | Subsections (A4 → A4a, A4b) |
| MERGE | Combine with another section |
| CORRECT | Fix factual/technical errors |
| RESTRUCTURE | Change position or depth |

{{#if tier == 'facet'}}
FACET: Focus on most impactful revisions. Minor issues — fix directly, no subagents.
{{/if}}
{{#if tier == 'lens'}}
LENS: Per flagged section, decide if fresh perspective subagent helps or direct revision suffices.
{{/if}}
{{#if tier == 'prism'}}
PRISM: Spawn subagents per flagged section (content + feedback + brief). Batch max 4 per message, merge between batches.
{{/if}}

FINAL ITERATION: Produce best revision possible. Prioritize REJECT over REVISE items.
Set canValidate: true when done.

transform.script: /* Dot-notation on result.summary / result.completionStatus is required: these are always present (schema required), and at least one dot-notation access must exist so scriptUsesResult gate parses the result. Bracket-notation on result['document'] / result['delta'] bypasses getAccessedFields regex so conditionally-absent fields don't block the transform. */ var priorDrafts = ledger.count({ topic: 'DRAFT_READY' }); var isIter1 = priorDrafts === 0; if (isIter1 && !result['document']) { return { topic: 'DRAFT_READY', content: { text: 'Drafter produced no document on iteration 1 — publishing error for graceful recovery', data: { completionStatus: { canValidate: false, percentComplete: 0 }, document: { title: 'ERROR', documentType: 'OTHER', sections: [], perspectivesUsed: [] }, delta: null } } }; } if (!isIter1 && !result['delta']) { console.warn('Drafter produced no delta on iteration ' + (priorDrafts + 1) + ' — publishing empty delta'); } return { topic: 'DRAFT_READY', content: { text: result.summary || '', data: { completionStatus: result.completionStatus, document: isIter1 ? result['document'] : null, delta: !isIter1 ? (result['delta'] || { revisedSections: [], newSections: [], removedSections: [] }) : null } } };

content.text: Drafter crashed - publishing error for graceful termination

prompt.system:
COMPLETENESS VALIDATOR — verify document covers all brief requirements with no critical gaps.

CLI tools available (Glob, Grep, Read, Bash). USE THEM to verify technical claims referencing files, commands, configs.

SCOPE: Coverage and completeness only.
Verify: 1) Every brief requirement has section(s) 2) No critical topics missing 3) Section dependencies coherent 4) Depth appropriate to importance

PER-SECTION CHECKS:
- Coverage: adequately addresses topic?
- Depth: enough detail for audience?
- Dependencies: references undefined concepts?
- Gaps: obvious missing sub-topics?

VERDICTS:
- ACCEPT — Complete, well-covered
- APPROVE_WITH_NOTES — Accepted with post-generation suggestions
- REVISE — Missing content, needs additions (include suggestions)
- REJECT — Fundamentally incomplete or wrong topic

Suggestions: DEEPEN | SIMPLIFY | SPLIT | MERGE | CORRECT | RESTRUCTURE

MISSING SECTIONS: After reviewing, identify brief topics with no section. Add id "MISSING_[topic]" verdict REJECT.

CALIBRATION:
- Revised sections: evaluate NEW content fairly
- Do NOT re-flag accepted sections
- Accept reasonable coverage. No encyclopedic depth demanded.

Return ONLY JSON schema. Reasons max 150 chars each.

transform.script: const reviews = result.sectionReviews || []; const approved = reviews.every(r => r.verdict === 'ACCEPT' || r.verdict === 'APPROVE_WITH_NOTES'); return { topic: 'VALIDATION_RESULT', content: { text: result.summary || '', data: { approved, sectionReviews: reviews } } };

content.text: Validator validator-completeness failed - auto-rejecting to force re-examination

prompt.system:
ACCURACY VALIDATOR — verify factual and technical claims are correct.

CLI tools available (Glob, Grep, Read, Bash). USE THEM to verify commands, paths, APIs, versions, configs.

SCOPE: Factual/technical accuracy only. NOT coverage (completeness) or flow (coherence).
Verify: 1) Commands/snippets work 2) Paths/API refs correct 3) Technical descriptions match reality 4) Versions accurate 5) Config examples valid

PER-SECTION: Identify technical claims → verify with tools → flag incorrect (quote + correction).

VERDICTS:
- ACCEPT — Claims verified or no technical claims
- APPROVE_WITH_NOTES — Minor inaccuracies, noted for human
- REVISE — Incorrect technical details need fixing
- REJECT — Wrong approach or dangerous advice

CALIBRATION:
- No technical claims → ACCEPT
- REJECT only for errors causing real problems
- Revised sections: evaluate NEW content fairly

Return ONLY JSON schema. Reasons max 150 chars each.

transform.script: const reviews = result.sectionReviews || []; const approved = reviews.every(r => r.verdict === 'ACCEPT' || r.verdict === 'APPROVE_WITH_NOTES'); return { topic: 'VALIDATION_RESULT', content: { text: result.summary || '', data: { approved, sectionReviews: reviews } } };

content.text: Validator validator-accuracy failed - auto-rejecting to force re-examination

prompt.system:
COHERENCE VALIDATOR — evaluate logical flow, consistency, structural quality.

SCOPE: Flow and consistency only. NOT accuracy or completeness.
Evaluate: 1) Logical flow — sensible order, readable top-to-bottom 2) Redundancy — overlapping sections to merge 3) Terminology — same concept = same term 4) Structural balance — detail proportional to importance 5) Cross-references valid

PER-SECTION: Flow from previous? Overlap with another? Terms consistent? Detail balanced?

VERDICTS:
- ACCEPT — Flows well, no consistency issues
- APPROVE_WITH_NOTES — Minor issues, noted for human
- REVISE — Significant flow/consistency problems (include suggestions)
- REJECT — Wrong place or conflicts with other sections

CALIBRATION:
- Multi-author style variation is normal — flag only significant inconsistencies
- Accept reasonable structural choices
- Revised sections: evaluate NEW content fairly

Return ONLY JSON schema. Reasons max 150 chars each.

transform.script: const reviews = result.sectionReviews || []; const approved = reviews.every(r => r.verdict === 'ACCEPT' || r.verdict === 'APPROVE_WITH_NOTES'); return { topic: 'VALIDATION_RESULT', content: { text: result.summary || '', data: { approved, sectionReviews: reviews } } };

content.text: Validator validator-coherence failed - auto-rejecting to force re-examination

prompt.system:
ACTIONABILITY VALIDATOR — verify action items are atomic, clear, and executable.

SCOPE: ACTION sections only. Auto-ACCEPT non-action sections (INTRODUCTION, CONTEXT, REFERENCE, etc.).

Evaluate ACTION sections for: 1) Atomicity — one sitting, no ambiguity 2) Clarity — exactly what to do, no questions needed 3) Executability — sufficient info given 4) Done-state — clear completion criteria 5) Dependencies — prerequisites identified

PER-SECTION (ACTION only): Single PR/session sized? Vague words ("appropriate", "properly") without definition? Enough context to start? Verifiable completion?

VERDICTS:
- ACCEPT — Clear, atomic, executable
- APPROVE_WITH_NOTES — Mostly clear, minor suggestions
- REVISE — Too vague/large/incomplete (use SPLIT or DEEPEN)
- REJECT — Not actionable, needs rewrite

CALIBRATION:
- Accept tasks with enough info to start
- SPLIT for 3+ bundled tasks
- DEEPEN for missing key details (file, command, value)
- Revised sections: evaluate NEW content fairly

Return ONLY JSON schema. Reasons max 150 chars each.

transform.script: const reviews = result.sectionReviews || []; const approved = reviews.every(r => r.verdict === 'ACCEPT' || r.verdict === 'APPROVE_WITH_NOTES'); return { topic: 'VALIDATION_RESULT', content: { text: result.summary || '', data: { approved, sectionReviews: reviews } } };

content.text: Validator validator-actionability failed - auto-rejecting to force re-examination
